metadata.subgroup_label: Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts

verse_context_groups.label: Formed thought-content and inner cognitive disposition (noēma, ennoia, epinoia, dianoēma, frēn)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the inner cognitive content — the mind's formed thoughts as they can be blinded, captured, corrupted, or guarded. Satan has designs (noēmata); unbelievers' minds are blinded (noēmata); every thought must be taken captive (noēma); the word of God discerns the intentions and thoughts of the heart (ennoia/enthumēsis); Simon's intent (epinoia) requires repentance. Inner-being phenomenon: formed inner thought-content and cognitive disposition.

anchor_verse.verse_text: 2Cor 4:4 In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers , to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ , who is the image of God .
anchor_verse.meaning: noēma names the inner cognitive faculty of unbelievers that the god of this world has blinded — the thought-mind rendered unable to see the light of the gospel.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'The god of this world has blinded the minds (noēmata) of the unbelievers, to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel.' The phenomenon at its most diagnostic: the noēma as the inner cognitive faculty that can be blinded — rendered unable to perceive the light of the gospel. The verse names the agent (the god of this world), the faculty (noēma), the action upon it (blinding), and its consequence (inability to see the gospel's glory).

verses.verse_text: Luk 11:17 But he , knowing their thoughts , said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and a divided household falls .
verses.meaning: dianoēma names the inner thoughts of the crowd that Jesus knows — the formed inner thought-content of persons that divine perception grasps directly.

verses.verse_text: Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .
verses.meaning: ennoia names the inner intentions or purposes of the heart as what the word of God discerns — the purposeful inner thought-dispositions alongside the enthumēsis cognitive thoughts.

verses.verse_text: 1Pe 4:1 Since therefore Christ suffered in the flesh , arm yourselves with the same way of thinking , for whoever has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin ,
verses.meaning: ennoia names the way of thinking or inner thought-orientation to be taken on — arming oneself with the same inner disposition that Christ had in suffering.

verses.verse_text: Act 8:22 Repent , therefore , of this wickedness of yours , and pray to the Lord that, if possible , the intent of your heart may be forgiven you .
verses.meaning: epinoia names the purposeful inner intent of Simon's heart — the formed inner thought-disposition that animated his offer of money for the Spirit. This intent requires repentance and forgiveness.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .
verses.meaning: noēma names Satan's designs or thought-schemes — the formed inner strategy of the adversary that the apostolic community knows and guards against.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 3:14 But their minds were hardened . For to this day , when they read the old covenant , that same veil remains unlifted , because only through Christ is it taken away .
verses.meaning: noēma names the minds of those who read the old covenant — the inner cognitive faculty in its state of hardening, unable to receive what the covenant communicates.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:4 In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers , to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ , who is the image of God .
verses.meaning: noēma names the inner cognitive faculty of unbelievers that the god of this world has blinded — the thought-mind rendered unable to see the light of the gospel.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 10:5 We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God , and take every thought captive to obey Christ ,
verses.meaning: noēma names the individual formed thought as what must be captured — the inner cognitive content brought under the authority of Christ.

verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:3 But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning , your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ .
verses.meaning: noēma names the inner thoughts that can be corrupted — the formed cognitive content that the serpent's cunning may redirect away from Christ.

verses.verse_text: Phili 4:7 And the peace of God , which surpasses all understanding , will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus .
verses.meaning: noēma names the minds that God's peace will guard — the inner cognitive faculty held secure by divine peace in Christ.

verses.verse_text: 1Cor 14:20 Brothers , do not be children in your thinking . Be infants in evil , but in your thinking be mature .
verses.meaning: frēn names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for rational and moral discernment that marks adult inner life. To be children in thinking is to be undeveloped in the faculty; to be mature is to have it trained and functioning at full capacity.

verse_context_groups.label: Inner formed thoughts — arising, alarming, and discerned (ra.yon, re.a, ash.tut, esh.to.nah)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names the formed thoughts that arise in the mind — Nebuchadnezzar's night-thoughts producing his dream, Belshazzar's alarming thoughts at the writing on the wall, Daniel's alarming thoughts held inwardly after the vision, God discerning the psalmist's thoughts from afar, and the precious thoughts of God himself. Inner-being phenomenon: arising formed thought-content.

anchor_verse.verse_text: Psa 139:17 How precious to me are your thoughts , O God ! How vast is the sum of them!
anchor_verse.meaning: re.a names God's thoughts as the object of the psalmist's wondering — divine inner thought-content is precious and vast beyond counting. The same word that named the psalmist's thoughts known by God is now applied to God's own thoughts that are precious to the psalmist.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'How precious to me are your thoughts, O God! How vast is the sum of them!' The same word (re.a) used in v.2 for the psalmist's thoughts discerned by God is here applied to God's own thoughts precious to the psalmist. The reflexivity reveals the phenomenon: thought-content as an inner reality that both creature and Creator hold, and that relates them to each other.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:29 To you , O king , as you lay in bed came thoughts of what would be after this , and he who reveals mysteries made known to you what is to be .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Nebuchadnezzar's inner thoughts arising in the night — the formed thought-content produced by the king's mind in sleep, which produced the dream.

verses.verse_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names the king's mind-thoughts as what the revelation will make known — the formed inner content of the royal mind is the object of the interpretation.

verses.verse_text: Dan 4:19 Then Daniel , whose name was Belteshazzar , was dismayed for a while , and his thoughts alarmed him. The king answered and said , “ Belteshazzar , let not the dream or the interpretation alarm you.” Belteshazzar answered and said , “My lord , may the dream be for those who hate you and its interpretation for your enemies !
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Daniel's formed inner thoughts in response to the dream — the thought-content arising in his mind at the revelation alarms him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:6 Then the king’s color changed , and his thoughts alarmed him; his limbs gave way , and his knees knocked together .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Belshazzar's inner thoughts produced by seeing the writing — the formed inner content of fear that immediately produces physical collapse.

verses.verse_text: Dan 5:10 The queen , because of the words of the king and his lords , came into the banqueting hall , and the queen declared , “O king , live forever ! Let not your thoughts alarm you or your color change .
verses.meaning: ra.yon names the alarming thoughts the queen counsels the king not to let disturb him — the inner formed thought-content of fear that she urges should not master him.

verses.verse_text: Dan 7:28 “ Here is the end of the matter . As for me , Daniel , my thoughts greatly alarmed me, and my color changed , but I kept the matter in my heart .”
verses.meaning: ra.yon names Daniel's inner thoughts produced by the vision — the formed thought-content of the revelation that disturbs and must be held inwardly.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:2 You know when I sit down and when I rise up ; you discern my thoughts from afar .
verses.meaning: re.a names the psalmist's inner thoughts as what God discerns from afar — the formed inner content of the person's mind is fully transparent to divine knowing.

verses.verse_text: Psa 139:17 How precious to me are your thoughts , O God ! How vast is the sum of them!
verses.meaning: re.a names God's thoughts as the object of the psalmist's wondering — divine inner thought-content is precious and vast beyond counting. The same word that named the psalmist's thoughts known by God is now applied to God's own thoughts that are precious to the psalmist.

verses.verse_text: Job 12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune ; it is ready for those whose feet slip .
verses.meaning: ash.tut names the inner thought or attitude of the comfortable person — the formed inner disposition of those at ease, from which contempt for the suffering arises. Ease-induced inner contempt.

verses.verse_text: Psa 146:4 When his breath departs , he returns to the earth ; on that very day his plans perish .
verses.meaning: esh.to.nah names the plans that cease at death — the formed inner thoughts and purposes that belong to the living person and vanish the moment life ends.

verse_context_groups.label: Knowledge as formed inner learning and cognitive content (mad.da)
verse_context_groups.context_description: The term names knowledge as formed inner content — what Solomon requested from God (wisdom and knowledge), what God placed in the four Danielic youths, what is active even in private thoughts. The phenomenon is knowledge as an inner formed content that can be given by God, held within the person, and exercised in service of governance and wisdom. Inner-being phenomenon: formed inner knowledge-content.

anchor_verse.verse_text: 2Ch 1:11 God answered Solomon , “ Because this was in your heart , and you have not asked for possessions , wealth , honor , or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life , but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people over whom I have made you king ,
anchor_verse.meaning: mad.da names the knowledge that was the content of Solomon's heart-request — God identifies what was in Solomon's heart as the basis for granting it. The knowledge-content of the request is exactly what was in the inner person.
anchor_verse.anchor_rationale: 'Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked for possessions...but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people.' God identifies the knowledge-request as something already in Solomon's heart before he asked — an inner orientation that shaped what he sought. The verse shows mad.da as inner content already present as a heart-disposition and then granted as a divine gift.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:10 Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people , for who can govern this people of yours, which is so great ?”
verses.meaning: mad.da names knowledge as the inner capacity Solomon requests for wise governance — he asks for it alongside wisdom as the inner equipment required for the task.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:11 God answered Solomon , “ Because this was in your heart , and you have not asked for possessions , wealth , honor , or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life , but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people over whom I have made you king ,
verses.meaning: mad.da names the knowledge that was the content of Solomon's heart-request — God identifies what was in Solomon's heart as the basis for granting it. The knowledge-content of the request is exactly what was in the inner person.

verses.verse_text: 2Ch 1:12 wisdom and knowledge are granted to you. I will also give you riches , possessions , and honor , such as none of the kings had who were before you, and none after you shall have the like .”
verses.meaning: mad.da names knowledge as what God grants in response — the inner capacity Solomon requested is now given as divine gift.

verses.verse_text: Ecc 10:20 Even in your thoughts , do not curse the king , nor in your bedroom curse the rich , for a bird of the air will carry your voice , or some winged creature tell the matter .
verses.meaning: mad.da names the inner thought-life or consciousness as the moral seat — even private thoughts carry moral accountability. The word serves here as a reference to inner conscious life.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:4 youths without blemish , of good appearance and skillful in all wisdom , endowed with knowledge , understanding learning , and competent to stand in the king’s palace , and to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans .
verses.meaning: mad.da names learning as one of the inner qualities of the chosen youths — the formed knowledge held within that enables them to serve in the royal court.

verses.verse_text: Dan 1:17 As for these four youths , God gave them learning and skill in all literature and wisdom , and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams .
verses.meaning: mad.da names learning as a God-given inner endowment — the formed knowledge-content that God placed within the four youths.
